detail:
Genuine, minor (cosmetic) regression introduced by the fix. Verified against the git diff of commit c1124d2 (the Phase-3 fix commit).

What the fix did: it renamed `isRevoked` (which matched only "revoked"/"error") to `needsReconnect` and added "expired", then reused that single predicate in THREE places â€” description, button label, AND the `badgeStatus` ternary (page.tsx:601-607). Pre-fix `badgeStatus` was `isActive ? "active" : isRevoked ? "error" : isConnected ? (integration?.status ?? "pending") : "pending"`. An "expired" record (isActive=false, isRevoked=false, isConnected=true) fell into the `isConnected` branch and rendered the raw status "expired", which StatusPill maps to AMBER (status-pill.tsx:23). Post-fix, "expired" is now caught by `needsReconnect` and `badgeStatus` becomes "error", which StatusPill maps to ROSE (status-pill.tsx:24). So the expired badge went amber â†’ rose. The same collapse hits "revoked": pre-fix it rendered raw "revoked" â†’ SLATE (status-pill.tsx:22); post-fix it becomes "error" â†’ rose. Three distinct StatusPill tones (expired=amber, revoked=slate, error=rose) are now collapsed into one rose tone for the non-active states.

This contradicts the defect's own scope: the FA-0362 detail explicitly says "the StatusPill correctly shows 'expired' in amber" and the suggested_fix said only to change the line-587 reconnect predicate so the description/button match the badge â€” it did NOT ask to touch badge logic. The intended end state (per the defect) was description "Reconnection required" + button "Reconnect" while KEEPING the amber expired badge. The fix instead changed the badge too.

User impact: real but small. An expired/revoked integration now shows a rose "error" pill instead of its intended amber "expired" / slate "revoked" pill, losing the design system's deliberate visual distinction between "lapsed/needs-reconnect" (amber) and "hard error" (rose). No functional/data impact â€” description, button, and OAuth recovery flow all work correctly. Note the underlying defect (description/button mismatch) WAS correctly fixed; only the badge color is a side-effect regression. Severity low (cosmetic, design-token level).
suggested_fix: Don't reuse `needsReconnect` for the badge. Keep the badge driven by the raw status so StatusPill renders its per-status tone. E.g. set `badgeStatus = isActive ? "active" : isConnected ? (integration?.status ?? "pending") : "pending"` (drop the `needsReconnect ? "error"` arm entirely, since StatusPill already maps revokedâ†’slate, expiredâ†’amber, errorâ†’rose). Leave the description and button predicates using `needsReconnect`. This restores the amber expired / slate revoked badges while preserving the FA-0362 description/button fix.
evidence: git show c1124d2 -- frontend/app/settings/integrations/page.tsx: badgeStatus arm changed from `: isRevoked ? "error"` to `: needsReconnect ? "error"` â€” pulls expired/revoked out of the raw-status fallthrough
frontend/app/settings/integrations/page.tsx:601-607 â€” badgeStatus maps {revoked,error,expired} all to "error" via needsReconnect
frontend/components/status-pill.tsx:22-24 â€” revoked=slate, expired=amber, error=rose (three distinct tones now collapsed to rose)
DEFECTS.json FA-0362 detail (line 1194): 'the StatusPill correctly shows expired in amber (status-pill.tsx:23)' â€” defect declared the amber badge already correct/in-scope-excluded
DEFECTS.json FA-0362 suggested_fix (line 1195): only asks to change line-587 reconnect predicate for description/button; 'No backend change needed' â€” badge logic not in scope
Pre-fix flow confirmed: expired => isActive=false,isRevoked=false,isConnected=true => isConnected branch => raw 'expired' => amber
